story: Boo! Al Lewis, the actor who'd become famous as Grandpa on 'The Munsters,' was born in 1923. On April 30, Brooklyn welcomed this future TV ghoul. Lewis would grow up to charm audiences with his spooky-yet-lovable vampire character, making families laugh for generations.

title: Hockey Hero Jackie McLeod's Birthday
story: Skates sliced the ice as Jackie McLeod first entered the world on April 30, 1930. Born in Regina, Saskatchewan, he grew up to become a hockey superstar. McLeod played right wing, winning gold at the World Championship in 1961. He even skated for the famous New York Rangers! Later, Jackie coached Team Canada to Olympic bronze in 1968.
activity_question: What position did Jackie McLeod play in hockey?
bonus_fact: Jackie McLeod's hometown, Regina, is the capital city of Saskatchewan, Canada.

story: Laughter filled the air as Bernie Clifton took his first breath in St Helens, Lancashire, England. Born in 1936 on April 30, this future comedian would bring joy to millions. title: Sci-Fi Star Born: Larry Niven
story: Blast off into the world of science fiction! Larry Niven, a stellar author, entered our universe on April 30, 1938, in Los Angeles. He grew up to write mind-bending stories about space and technology. Niven's talent for crafting alien worlds and futuristic adventures earned him five prestigious Hugo Awards. His famous work 'Neutron Star' continues to inspire readers and fellow writers.

title: A Creative Mind Born
story: Pencils and cameras at the ready! David King, a talented artist with many skills, entered the world in 1943. Born in Isleworth, England on April 30, King would grow up to design album covers for rock legends like Jimi Hendrix and The Who. He also snapped photos and wrote history books, leaving a colorful mark on art and culture.

title: Merrill Osmond: A Musical Birthday
story: Pop music got a little sweeter in 1953 when Merrill Osmond was born in Ogden, Utah. He grew up to become a famous singer and songwriter with the Osmond Brothers. Their catchy tunes like 'One Bad Apple' and 'Down by the Lazy River' had people dancing and singing along. Merrill's April 30 birthday marked the start of a melodious journey in American pop music.
activity_question: What famous group was Merrill Osmond part of?
bonus_fact: The Osmonds began as a barbershop quartet formed by Merrill's older brothers.

title: Reggae Star's Jamaican Roots
story: Barrington Levy's powerful voice first echoed across Jamaica in 1964. Born on April 30 in Clarendon, this future reggae and dancehall sensation would grow up to create unforgettable rhythms. Levy's music, including his hit 'Englishman', would later spread Jamaican sounds around the world.
